question.label: Ticket expectations
question.name: What feels clear or unclear about what “done enough to ship” means here?
question.description: Checks whether his hesitation is coming from expectations rather than ability.

answer: I usually wait too long before asking because I want to solve things myself first.

assessment.note: He describes delaying help-seeking because he is trying to solve things alone first, which strongly signals unclear shipping expectations and escalation norms.

new_queue.label: Ask earlier
new_queue.name: When you hold off asking, what tells you it is time to pull someone in?

new_queue.label: Confidence trigger
new_queue.name: What tends to make you pause before shipping a ticket?

new_queue.label: Waiting on piece
new_queue.name: What are you usually waiting on when work starts to feel stuck?

new_queue.label: Support cadence
new_queue.name: What kind of check-in from me would be most useful over the next two weeks?

new_queue.label: Two-week takeaway
new_queue.name: What’s the one thing you want to have changed by our next check-in?

issues: coverage: injected engagement into next question (0 touches after turn 4)

history.answer_excerpt: I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
Local setup is better now, but I still lose time when the design and API details do not match.
I am not always sure who should approve what before I push.
I usually wait too long before asking because I want to solve things myself first.
